old_claim.note: Public Education Systems provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: The documented club arose from voluntary experimenters and does not require a public education system as a direct cause.

source_shape.source_locator: ARRL committee report Appendix A, page 10: records 1909 as the year the first radio clubs started, before U.S. amateur licensing began in 1912.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: ARRL's historical timeline records the start of the first radio clubs in 1909.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The scoped evidence establishes the milestone without the removed dependency. The documented club arose from voluntary experimenters and does not require a public education system as a direct cause.

ontology_before: amateur_radio_communities treated public_education_systems as a direct causal prerequisite.
ontology_after: amateur_radio_communities is scoped to the directly evidenced mechanism and no longer claims public_education_systems as a prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-12-structural-accuracy-batch: amateur_radio_communities no longer directly depends on public_education_systems.
would_reject_if: A reliable source demonstrates that every implementation within the current amateur_radio_communities scope technically requires public_education_systems.
short_reason: The documented club arose from voluntary experimenters and does not require a public education system as a direct cause.
